what_it_does: Defines a voice system as a structural counterpart to visual design records, listing invariant verbal traits, verbal forms, binding targets, verbal laws, and lint categories.
observations: States that every verbal form binds to a visual twin.
Names five registers: immersive, tension, explanatory, credible, precise.
Defines invariant voice tokens including present tense, active voice, first-person plural, telegraphic phrasing, gerund mission language, sentence-like numbers, and candor.
Provides a table of verbal archetypes with shape, word budget, and design binding.
Maps three verbal laws to rationing, differentiation by register, and local resolution.
Describes lint as predicate checks plus model judgments.
standouts: The file treats voice as part of the same record system as visual design.
The forms table connects language units to specific layout or component counterparts.
The lint section frames examples as self-checking material.
conventions: Uses Markdown headings, bullets, emphasis, inline code, and a compact table.
Uses design-system vocabulary for verbal behavior: tokens, forms, registers, slots, bindings, lint, nearest-scope-wins.
Some table rows use explicit word counts while others use an em dash.
concerns: Several forms do not specify a word budget despite the table having a words column.
The registers are named but not defined in this file.
The lint proof references external artifacts that are not included here.
notes: Briefing note: this file specifies how voice behaves as a design-system face with visual bindings, form budgets, and lintable constraints.
questions: What are the exact behavioral differences among the five registers?
Which examples are used by the self-lint proof?
What determines when a form-level rule outranks a page-level register default?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents voice as the same design DNA expressed verbally and structures that claim into invariants, forms, laws, and lint.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make brand voice operational by turning cadence, phrasing, and register choices into recordable rules tied to visual counterparts.
novelty: The file frames verbal style as a face of the same substrate as visual design, with each language form mapped to a visual twin and lint category.
contribution: Contributes a neutral voice ledger: invariant verbal tokens, archetypal forms, word budgets, visual bindings, register logic, and lint modes for integrating language behavior into a fused design/code substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This Markdown document defines a structured voice system whose verbal forms, invariants, registers, visual bindings, laws, and lint checks describe how spoken brand DNA operates.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make voice rules explicit, composable, and checkable within the same record logic used for visual design.
